name: Rostov Oblast (Russia)

colors.symbolism: the unity of the Don land with the Russian state

description: Three horizontal stripes of blue, yellow and red of equal height, overlaid by a white vertical stripe along the hoist occupying about one-ninth of the flag's length.
history: Adopted on 28 October 1996 by the Legislative Assembly of Rostov Oblast; the blue-yellow-red tricolour reproduces the historic flag of the Don Cossack Host (1918), while the white hoist stripe was added to symbolise the union of the Don land with Russia.

features.symbolism: the white hoist stripe added in 1996 to represent the union of the Don land with the Russian state
